reasoning_model_1: Model 1 generated the content 'If you agree, then we will start.' which matches the script perfectly (Content Accuracy: 3). Regarding the 'Pause' dimension, the prompt required a specific pause after the word 'if'. The transcript shows a clean text generation without hallucinating instructions. Based on the timestamp duration (3s) and the clean text, it is inferred that the model integrated the prosodic feature correctly or at least maintained naturalness without verbalizing the command. Regarding 'Volume', the speech is delivered at a normal level as requested, meeting the requirement.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 failed significantly in Content Accuracy. The transcript 'If pause you agree...' (00:00-00:01) indicates that the model verbalized the instruction word 'pause' instead of executing the silence. This is a critical error where the control tag was leaked into the speech. Consequently, for the 'Pause' dimension, the model scores 0 as it did the opposite of the paralinguistic request (spoke the word instead of pausing). Regarding 'Volume', evaluating independently of the content error, the speech was likely produced at a normal volume, fulfilling that specific dimension's requirement.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately synthesized the text while likely adhering to the paralinguistic instruction (Pause) or at least maintaining correct content. Model 2 suffered from a severe instruction-following failure, verbalizing the command 'pause' as part of the sentence ('If pause you agree'), which ruins the 'Pause' dimension performance. Both models performed equally on the 'Volume' dimension, as there were no indications of volume abnormalities.
